Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub struct SearchQuery {\n41\t    #[serde(flatten)]\n42\t    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\t/// Canary run result\n46\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n47\tpub struct CanaryRunResult {\n48\t    pub canary_id: String,\n49\t    pub ran_at: i64,\n50\t    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n51\t    pub latency_ms: i64,\n52\t    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n53\t    pub hit_count: usize,\n54\t    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\t/// Canary run status\n58\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize,    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n110\t        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n111\t\n112\t        loop {\n113\t            interval.tick().await;\n114\t            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n115\t                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n116\t            }\n117\t        }\n118\t    }\n119\t\n120\t    /// Run all canaries that are due\n121\t    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n122\t        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n123\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n124\t\n125\t        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n126\t        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n127\t        for canary in canaries {\n128\t            if !canary.enabled {\n129\t                continue;\n130\t            }\n131\t\n132\t            // Check if already running\n133\t            let running = self.running.read().await;\n134\t            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n135\t                continue;\n136\t            }\n137\t            drop(running);\n138\t\n139\t            // Check last run time\n140\t            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n141\t            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n142\t                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n143\t                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n144\t            } else {\n145\t                true\n146\t            };\n147\t\n148\t            if should_run {\n149\t                due_canaries.push(canary);\n150\t            }\n151\t        }\n152\t\n153\t        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n154\t        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n155\t            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n156\t            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n157\t\n158\t            // Mark as running\n159\t            {\n160\t                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n161\t                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n162\t            }\n163\t\n164\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n165\t                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n166\t\n167\t                // Mark as not running\n168\t                {\n169\t                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n170\t                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n171\t                }\n172\t\n173\t                if let Err(e) = result {\n174\t                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n175\t                }\n176\t            });\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        Ok(())\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Run a single canary\n183\t    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n184\t        let start = Instant::now();\n185\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n186\t\n187\t        // Parse query\n188\t        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n189\t            .map_err(|e| serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n193\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n194\t\n195\t        // Execute the search self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n199\t\n200\t        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n201\t\n202\t        // Evaluate assertions\n203\t        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n204\t        for assertion in &assertions {\n205\t            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n206\t                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t\n210\t        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n211\t            CanaryStatus::Passed\n212\t        } else {\n213\t            CanaryStatus::Failed\n214\t        };\n215\t\n216\t        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n217\t            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n218\t            ran_at: now,\n219\t            status,\n220\t            latency_ms,\n221\t            failed_assertions,\n222\t            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n223\t            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n224\t        };\n225\t\n226\t        // Store the run\n227\t        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n228\t            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n229\t            ran_at: now,\n230\t            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n231\t            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n232\t            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n233\t                None\n234\t            } else {\n235\t                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n236\t            },\n237\t        };\n238\t\n239\t        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n240\t\n241\t        // Emit metrics\n242\t        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n243\t\n244\t        Ok(result)\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    /// Execute a search query against the index\n248\t    async fn execute_search(&self, _index_uid: &str, _query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n249\t        // TODO: Wire this to the actual search client\n250\t        // For       query: String::new(),\n256\t        })\n257\t    }\n258\t\n259\t    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n260\t    fn evaluate_assertion(\n261\t        &self,\n262\t        assertion: CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n268\t                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n269\t                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n270\t     .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n286\t                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n287\t                    .collect();\n288\t\n289\t                let missing: Vec<_> =                     message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n309\t                    });\n310\t                }\n311\t            }\n312\t            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n313\t                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n314\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n315\t                        assertion_type: CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n323\t                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n324\t                // For now, this assertion always passes\n325\t            }\n326\t            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n327\t                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n328\t                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n329\t Body: `{type: \"click_through\" \\| \"latency\", event_id: <uuid>, query: <string>, result_id?: <string>, position?: <int>, duration_ms?: <int>, timestamp: <int ms>}`. Auth: same JWT as other search UI endpoints (`Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` with `beacon` scope — see §13.21 JWT claims). Rate-limited per `search_ui.rate_limit` (shares the `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>` bucket with queries). Emits a CDC event with `type: click_through` (always emitted) or `type: latency` (subject to `cdc.emit_internal_writes`). `event_id` is used as the dedup key in the idempotency cache (matches the §13.10 idempotency pattern without requiring the `Idempotency-Key` header — `event_id` plays the same role). See §13.21. |\n886\t| `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key` | Manually trigger scoped-key rotation for the search UI (normally automatic `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` before expiry). Body: `{force: boolean}` — `force=true` bypasses the `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` timing gate and rotates immediately regardless of how recent the current key is; `force=false` (or omitted) honors the timing gate and is a no-op if the current key is not yet within the rotation window. Auth: admin-gated (`Authorization: Bearer <admin_key>` or `X-Admin-Key`). Response: `{old_key_id, new_key_id, generation, rotated_at}`. See §13.21 \"Scoped-key rotation coordination\". |\n887\t| **Admin UI (§13.19)** | |\n888\t| `GET /_miroir/admin` | Embedded single-page admin UI. Static assets served from `rust-embed`. |\n889\t| `POST /_miroir/admin/login` | Exchange admin credentials for a session cookie. Body: `{admin_key}`. On success sets `Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=<sealed>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict` (sealed with `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` — see §9) and returns `{csrf_token}`; the session row is persisted to `admin_sessions` so logout on any pod is visible to all pods. Rate limit 10/minute per source IP, backed by `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` when `miroir.replicas > 1` (required — Helm `values.schema.json` rejects local-only admin login rate-limiting in HA). Failed-login exponential backoff: after 5 consecutive failures from the same IP, the backoff window doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, …) up to a 24h cap, tracked in `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>`; a successful login resets both counters. |\n890\t| `POST /_miroir/admin/logout` | Invalidates the session cookie server-side (revokes the sealed session ID) and clears it client-side (`Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=; Max-Age=0`). |\n891\t| **Public UI (§13.21)** | |\n892\t| `GET /ui/search/{index}` | Embedded single-page search UI for end users. This is the one public SPA entry point served outside `/_miroir/`. |\n893\t\n894\t**Client API extensions.** Two client-facing additions sit alongside the admin table above but are **not** admin-gated — they use the normal `master_key` authorization:\n895\t\n896\t- `POST /multi-search` — batch query API (§13.11). Body: `{\"queries\": [ {indexUid, q, ...}, ... ]}`. Each query is scattered independently and returned in input order.\n897\t- `POST /indexes/{uid}/explain` — query plan API (§13.20).    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n138\t) -> Result<Json<CanaryStatusResponse>, StatusCode>\n139\twhere\n140\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n141\t    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n142\t{\n143\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a99e8ba487034a83b3d76a91","type":"tool_result","content":"130\t1. **Per-group quorum.** A group is successful for a shard when at least `floor(RF/2) + 1` of its RF target nodes ACK enqueue. (RF=1 → 1 ACK; RF=2 → 2 ACKs; RF=3 → 2 ACKs; RF=4 → 3 ACKs.)\n131\t2. **Write success.** The write returns HTTP 200 if **at least one group** met its per-group quorum for the shard. The response carries `X-Miroir-Degraded` whenever **any** group did not meet its quorum for any shard in the batch (listing the affected groups and shards). HTTP 503 with `miroir_no_quorum` is returned only when **no group** met its per-group quorum for a given shard.\n132\t\n133\tExamples:\n134\t\n135\t| Topology | Per-group quorum | Write succeeds when |\n136\t|----------|------------------|---------------------|\n137\t| RG=1, RF=1 | 1 of 1 | that one node ACKs |\n138\t| RG=1, RF=2 | 2 of 2 | both replicas ACK |\n139\t| RG=1, RF=3 | 2 of 3 | any 2 of the 3 replicas ACK |\n140\t| RG=2, RF=1 | 1 of 1 per group | at least one of the two groups' single node ACKs |\n141\t| RG=2, RF=3 | 2 of 3 per group | either group reaches 2-of-3 ACKs |\n142\t\n143\tDocuments whose replicas did not ACK are not lost: any group that accepted the write becomes the authoritative source, and the anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) subsequently repairs any replica that missed the write on the next pass. This composition — best-effort fan-out, per-shard success when at least one group reaches quorum, plus continuous reconciliation — is the long-term safety net and the concrete expression of Design Principle 5 (graceful degradation) for the write path. This not-lost guarantee depends on `anti_entropy.enabled: true` (§13.8, default on). When anti-entropy is disabled, writes that don't reach every replica remain permanently divergent; operators running with anti-entropy off must either require full-replica-set writes (e.g., stricter client retries) or provide their own reconciliation.\n144\t\n145\t**`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name**: clients must not use a field with this name. Miroir rejects any document that already contains `_miroir_shard` with a `miroir_reserved_field` error.\n146\t\n147\t**Index settings and schema changes**: broadcast to all nodes in all groups. Index creation additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing.\n148\t\n149\t### Read path\n150\t\n151\t1. Client sends `POST /indexes/{index}/search`\n152\t2. Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\n153\t3. Within the selected group, build a **covering set**: one node per shard (round-robin across intra-group replicas for load balancing within the group)\n154\t4. Fan out the query to each node in the covering set in parallel, with `showRankingScore: true` appended\n155\t5. Each node executes a full Meilisearch search against its local document subset\n156\t6. **Result merge**: collect all hits, sort globally by `_rankingScore` descending, apply original `offset`/`limit`, strip `_rankingScore` if client did not request it, strip `_miroir_shard` always\n157\t7. **Facets**: sum per-value counts across the covering set nodes\n158\t8. **estimatedTotalHits**: sum across covering set nodes\n159\t9. **processingTimeMs**: report max across covering set nodes\n160\t\n161\tThe query only touches `N/RG` nodes (the nodes in the selected group), not the entire fleet. Adding more groups reduces the fan-out size per query and increases the number of queries that can run in parallel across the fleet.\n162\t\n163\t**Score comparability**: `_rankingScore` values are comparable across shards only if all nodes have identical ranking rules, typo tolerance, attribute weights, and synonyms. Miroir enforces this via the settings broadcast requirement. Because all groups receive identical settings changes, inter-group score comparability is also maintained (relevant for degraded-mode fallback, below).\n164\t\n165\t**Group unavailability fallback**: if the selected group has an unhealthy node and the shard's intra-group RF cannot cover it, Miroir can optionally fall back to a different group for that query. The result is still complete and correct — it just came from a different group. This is transparent to the client.\n166\t\n167\t**offset/limit**: each node must return up to `offset + limit` results. The orchestrator applies the real offset/limit after global merge.\n168\t\n169\t### Replica groups in practice\n170\t\n171\tReplica groups make read throughput a first-class scaling dimension, independent of storage capacity.\n172\t\n173\t```\n174\tExample: S=6 shards, RG=2 groups, RF=1 per group, 6 nodes total\n175\t\n176\tGroup 0: nodes [0, 1, 2]          Group 1: nodes [3, 4, 5]\n177\t  shard 0 → node 0                  shard 0 → node 3\n178\t  shard 1 → node 1                  shard 1 → node 4\n179\t  shard 2 → node 2                  shard 2 → node 5\n180\t  shard 3 → node 0                  shard 3 → node 3\n181\t  shard 4 → node 1                  shard 4 → node 4\n182\t  shard 5 → node 2                  shard 5 → node 5\n183\t\n184\tWrite for doc-X (shard 2): → node-2 (group 0) AND node-5 (group 1)  [2 writes]\n185\tQuery 1: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [3 node fan-out]\n186\tQuery 2: → group 1 → nodes [3, 4, 5]   [3 node fan-out]\n187\tQuery 3: → group 0 → nodes [0, 1, 2]   [queries 1 and 3 run fully in parallel]\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\tWith RG=2, two queries can execute simultaneously with zero resource contention. Adding a third group triples throughput, and so on.\n191\t\n192\t**Tradeoffs table**\n193\t\n194\t| RG | RF | Copies/doc | Read throughput | Storage per node | HA (within group) |\n195\t|----|-----|-----------|----------------|-----------------|------------------|\n196\t| 1 | 1 | 1 | 1× | full | none |\n197\t| 1 | 2 | 2 | 1× (replicas share load) | half | 1 node failure |\n198\t| 1 | 3 | 3 | 1× | third | 2 node failures |\n199\t| 2 | 1 | 2 | **2×** | half per group | none |\n200\t| 2 | 2 | 4 | **2×** | quarter per group | 1 node failure per group |\n201\t| 3 | 1 | 3 | **3×** | third per group | none |\n202\t| RG | RF | RG×RF | **RG×** | 1/(RG×RF) | RF−1 per group |\n203\t\n204\t`RF=1, RG=2` and `RF=2, RG=1` both store 2 copies of each document but behave differently:\n205\t- `RF=2, RG=1`: both copies in one group; each query uses that group (one query at a time per shard); tolerates 1 node failure within the group\n206\t- `RF=1, RG=2`: one copy in each group; queries alternate between groups (two fully parallel query streams); no intra-group HA\n207\t\n208\tChoose RG for throughput, RF for HA. They compose independently.\n209\t\n210\t**When to add a group vs. add a node to an existing group:**\n211\t\n212\t- Add a node to an existing group when the bottleneck is **per-node storage or indexing CPU** — the rebalancer distributes shards more finely within the group, each node holds fewer documents\n213\t- Add a new group when the bottleneck is **search query throughput** — the new group handles a separate stream of queries independently; existing groups are unaffected\n214\t\n215\t### Topology changes\n216\t\n217\tAll topology operations are scoped to a single replica group. Changes to one group do not affect other groups' data or query routing.\n218\t\n219\t**Adding a node to an existing group (capacity scaling; movement: ~1/(Ng+1) of that group's documents):**\n220\t1. Assign the new node to a group (config `replica_group` field); mark `joining`\n221\t2. Recompute shard assignments within that group — approximately `S/(Ng+1)` shards now prefer the new node\n222\t3. Dual-write: new inbound writes for affected shards in this group go to both the old owner and the new node\n223\t4. Background migration: for each affected shard, use `GET /indexes/{uid}/documents?filter=_miroir_shard={id}&limit=1000&offset=...` to page through only that shard's documents on the source node; write each page to the new node\n224\t5. Once all pages are transferred, mark node `active`; stop dual-write; delete migrated shard from old node with `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`\n225\t\n226\tDocuments on unaffected shards, and all documents in other groups, are never touched.\n227\t\n228\t**Adding a new replica group (throughput scaling; no migration of existing groups):**\n229\t1. Provision new nodes and assign them to a new group ID in config"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_31e0b76d3e144afcb112d655","session_id":"c278b2b0-d41b-493d-8c12-dc91cdad82aa","uuid":"a02c451e-35dc-4547-92a3-6ef9268bade1","timestamp":"2026-05-03T22:35:36.510Z"}

   pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n110\t        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n111\t\n112\t        loop {\n113\t            interval.tick().await;\n114\t            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n115\t                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n116\t            }\n117\t        }\n118\t    }\n119\t\n120\t    /// Run all canaries that are due\n121\t    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n122\t        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n123\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n124\t\n125\t        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n126\t        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n127\t        for canary in canaries {\n128\t            if !canary.enabled {\n129\t                continue;\n130\t            }\n131\t\n132\t            // Check if already running\n133\t            let running = self.running.read().await;\n134\t            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n135\t                continue;\n136\t            }\n137\t            drop(running);\n138\t\n139\t            // Check last run time\n140\t            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n141\t            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n142\t                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n143\t                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n144\t            } else {\n145\t                true\n146\t            };\n147\t\n148\t            if should_run {\n149\t                due_canaries.push(canary);\n150\t            }\n151\t        }\n152\t\n153\t        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n154\t        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n155\t            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n156\t            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n157\t\n158\t            // Mark as let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n170\t                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n171\t                }\n172\t\n173\t                if let Err(e) = result {\n174\t                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n175\t                }\n176\t            });\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        Ok(())\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Run a single canary\n183\t    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n184\t        let start = Instant::now();\n185\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n186\t\n187\t        // Parse query\n188\t        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n189\t            .map_err(|e| query against the index\n196\t        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n197\t        // For now, we simulate a search response\n198\t        let search_response = failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n204\t        for assertion in &assertions {\n205\t            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n206\t                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n207\t            }\n208\t        }\n209\t\n210\t        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n211\t            CanaryStatus::Passed\n212\t        } else {\n213\t            CanaryStatus::Failed\n214\t        };\n215\t\n216\t        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n217\t            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n218\t            ran_at: now,\n219\t            status,\n220\t            latency_ms,\n221\t            failed_assertions,\n222\t            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n223\t            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n224\t        };\n225\t\n226\t        // Store the run\n227\t        let new_run = Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n236\t            },\n237\t        };\n238\t\n239\t        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, &CanaryAssertion,\n263\t        response: &SearchResponse,\n264\t        latency_ms: i64,\n265\t    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n266\t        match assertion {\n267\t            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n268\t                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n269\t                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n270\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n271\t\n272\t                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n273\t                if actual != value {\n274\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n275\t                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n276\t                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n277\t                        actual:     .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n286\t                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n287\t                    .collect();\n288\t\n289\t                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n290\t                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n291\t                    .collect();\n292\t\n293\t                if !missing.is_empty() {\n294\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n295\t                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n296\t                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n297\t                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n298\t                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n299\t                    });\n300\t                }\n301\t            }\n302\t            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n303\t                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n304\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n305\t                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n306\t                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n307\t                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n308\t CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n313\t                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n314\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n315\t                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n316\t                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n317\t                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n318\t CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n323\t                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n324\t                // For now, this assertion always passes\n325\t            }\n326\t            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n327\t                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n328\t                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n329\t                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n330\t                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n331\t                        .unwrap_or(false));\n332\t\n333\t                if contains  });\n340\t                }\n341\t            }\n342\t        }\n343\t        None\n344\t    }\n345\t\n346\t    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n347\t    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: );\n357\t            }\n358\t            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n359\t                tracing::warn!(\n360\t                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n361\t                    latency_ms = CanaryStatus::Error => {\n367\t                tracing::error!(\n368\t                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n369\t                    \"Canary error\"\n370\t                );\n371\t            }\n372\t        }\n373\t    }\n374\t\n375\t    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n376\t    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n377\t        Self {\n378\t            store: }\n383\t    }\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t/// Create a canary from a definition\n387\tpub fn create_canary(\n388\t    id: String,\n389\t    name: String,\n390\t    index_uid: String,\n391\t    interval_s: index_uid,\n440\t            query,\n441\t            response,\n442\t            timestamp: now,\n443\t        });\n444\t\n445\t        // Keep only the most recent queries\n446\t        let len = queries.len();\n447\t        if len > self.max_queries {\n448\t            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n449\t        }\n450\t    }\n451\t\n452\t    /// Get captured queries\n453\t    pub #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>, max_concurrent: usize, run_history_limit: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, _index_uid: &str, _query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // TODO: Wire this to the actual search client\n        // For now, return a placeholder response\n        Ok(SearchResponse {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            estimated_total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            query: String::new(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n                // For now, this assertion always passes\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n    }\n}\n","numLines":462,"startLine":1,"totalLines":462}}}

§13.18 Canary routes\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRef, Path, State},\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Json,\n    routing::{delete, get, post, put},\n    Router,\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    canary::{Canary, CanaryAssertion, CanaryRunner, CapturedQuery, QueryCapture, SearchQuery},\n    error::MiroirError,\n    task_store::{CanaryRow, CanaryRunRow, NewCanary, TaskStore},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Canary management state.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct CanaryState {\n    pub store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    pub capture: Arc<QueryCapture>,\n}\n\n/// Canary creation request\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct CreateCanaryRequest {\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub query: serde_json::Value,\n    pub assertions: Vec<serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary status response\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CanaryStatusResponse {\n    pub canaries: Vec<CanaryInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CanaryInfo {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n    pub last_run: Option<CanaryRunInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunInfo {\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: usize,\n}\n\n/// Capture request\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct CaptureRequest {\n    pub max_queries: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n/// Capture response\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CaptureResponse {\n    pub capture_id: String,\n    pub queries: Vec<CapturedQueryInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CapturedQueryInfo {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: serde_json::Value,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\n/// Create a new canary\npub async fn create_canary<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Json(req): Json<CreateCanaryRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    // Parse query\n    let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_value(serde_json::json!(req.query))\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Invalid canary query\");\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        })?;\n\n    // Parse assertions\n    let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = req\n        .assertions\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|v| serde_json::from_value(v))\n        .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Invalid canary assertion\");\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        })?;\n\n    // Create NewCanary for storage\n    let new_canary = NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name: req.name.clone(),\n        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n        interval_s: req.interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query).unwrap_or_default(),\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions).unwrap_or_default(),\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    };\n\n    state.store.upsert_canary(&new_canary).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to store canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": id,\n        \"status\": \"created\"\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Get canary status\npub async fn get_canary_status<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n) -> Result<Json<CanaryStatusResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let canaries = state.store.list_canaries().map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to list canaries\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    let canary_infos: Vec<CanaryInfo> = canaries\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|canary| {\n            let runs = state.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1).unwrap_or_default();\n            let last_run = runs.first().map(|r| CanaryRunInfo {\n                ran_at: r.ran_at,\n                status: r.status.clone(),\n                latency_ms: r.latency_ms,\n                failed_assertions: r.failed_assertions_json.as_ref().map(|j| {\n        name: canary.name,\n                index_uid: canary.index_uid,\n                interval_s: canary.interval_s,\n                enabled: canary.enabled,\n                created_at: canary.created_at,\n                last_run,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(Json(CanaryStatusResponse {\n        canaries: canary_infos,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// Get a specific canary\npub async fn get_canary<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let canary = state.store.get_canary(&id).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to get canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?\n    .ok_or_else(|| StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    let runs = state.store.get_canary_runs(&id, 100).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": canary.id,\n        \"name\": canary.name,\n        \"index_uid\": canary.index_uid,\n        \"interval_s\": canary.interval_s,\n        \"enabled\": canary.enabled,\n        \"created_at\": canary.created_at,\n        \"query\": canary.query_json,\n        \"assertions\": canary.assertions_json,\n        \"runs\": runs\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Update a canary\npub async fn update_canary<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n    Json(req): Json<CreateCanaryRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    // Verify canary exists\n    let _existing = state.store.get_canary(&id).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to get canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?\n    .ok_or_else(|| StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    // Parse query\n    let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_value(serde_json::json!(req.query))\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Invalid canary query\");\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        })?;\n\n    // Parse assertions\n    let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = req\n        .assertions\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|v| serde_json::from_value(v))\n   query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query).unwrap_or_default(),\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions).unwrap_or_default(),\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: _existing.created_at,\n    };\n\n    state.store.upsert_canary(&new_canary).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to store canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": id,\n        \"status\": \"updated\"\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Delete a canary\npub async fn delete_canary<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<StatusCode, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    state.store.delete_canary(&id).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to delete canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(StatusCode::NO_CONTENT)\n}\n\n/// Start capturing queries for canary creation\npub async fn start_capture<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Json(req): Json<CaptureRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<CaptureResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let capture_id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n    let _max_queries = req.max_queries.unwrap_or(100);\n\n    // Clear previous captures\n    state.capture.clear().await;\n\n    Ok(Json(CaptureResponse {\n        capture_id,\n        queries: Vec::new(),\n    }))\n}\n\n/// Get captured queries\npub async fn get_captured<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let queries = state.capture.get_captured().await;\n\n    let query_infos: Vec<CapturedQueryInfo> = queries\n        .iter()\n        .map(|q| CapturedQueryInfo {\n            index_uid: q.index_uid.clone(),\n            query: serde_json::to_value(&q.query).unwrap_or_default(),\n            timestamp: q.timestamp,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"queries\": query_infos\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a captured query\npub async fn create_from_capture<S>(\n    State(state): queries = state.capture.get_captured().await;\n\n    // Find the first captured query matching the index UID\n    let captured = queries\n        .iter()\n        .find(|q| q.index_uid == index_uid)\n        .ok_or_else(|| StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    let id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n\n    // Create a default canary from the captured query\n    let canary = miroir_core::canary::create_canary(\n        id.clone(),\n        format!(\"canary-{}\", captured.index_uid),\n        captured.index_uid.clone(),\n        3600, // Default: run every hour\n        captured.query.clone(),\n        vec![\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value: 1 },\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value: 500 },\n        ],\n    ).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to create canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    state.store.put_canary(&canary).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to store canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": id,\n        \"status\": \"created\"\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Router Two-phase settings broadcast with verification (plan §13.5).\n//!\n//! This module implements the propose/verify/commit flow for settings changes,\n//! replacing the sequential apply-with-rollback approach.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Current phase of a settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum BroadcastPhase {\n    Idle = 0,\n    Propose = 1,\n    Verify = 2,\n    Commit = 3,\n}\n\n/// Status of an ongoing settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BroadcastStatus {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: BroadcastPhase,\n    /// Proposed settings fingerprint.\n    pub proposed_fingerprint: Option<String>,\n    /// Per-node task UIDs from Phase 1.\n    pub node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    /// Per-node verification results from Phase 2.\n    pub node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\n    /// Whether verification succeeded.\n    pub verify_ok: bool,\n    /// Settings version after commit.\n    pub settings_version: Option<u64>,\n    /// Error message if any.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Settings broadcast coordinator.\npub struct SettingsBroadcast {\n    /// In-flight broadcasts (index -> status).\n    in_flight: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, BroadcastStatus>>>,\n    /// Global settings version (incremented on successful commit).\n    settings_version: Arc<RwLock<u64>>,\n    /// Per-(index, node) settings version (for X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version).\n    node_settings_version: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<(String, String), u64>>>,\n}\n\nimpl SettingsBroadcast {\n    /// Create a new settings broadcast coordinator.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            in_flight: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),\n            node_settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current global settings version.\n    pub async fn current_version(&self) -> u64 {\n        *self.settings_version.read().await\n    }\n\n    /// Get the per-(index, node) settings version.\n    pub async fn node_version(&self, index: &str, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n        let versions = self.node_settings_version.read().await;\n        *versions.get(&(index.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).unwrap_or(&0)\n    }\n\n    /// Start a new settings broadcast (Phase 1: Propose).\n    ///\n    /// The caller should:\n    /// 1. PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings on each node in parallel\n    /// 2. Collect task_uid from each response\n    /// 3. Call `enter_verify` with the task UIDs\n    pub async fn start_propose(&self, index: String, settings: &Value) -> Result<String> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n\n        if in_flight.contains_key(&index) {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\n                \"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\",\n                index\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(settings);\n        let status = BroadcastStatus {\n            index: index.clone(),\n            phase: BroadcastPhase::Propose,\n            proposed_fingerprint: Some(fingerprint),\n            node_task_uids: HashMap::new(),\n            node_hashes: HashMap::new(),\n            verify_ok: false,\n            settings_version: None,\n            error: None,","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":365}}}

#[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub offset: Option<usize>,\n    /// Other query parameters.\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub other: HashMap<String, Value>,\n}\n\n/// Multi-search response.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MultiSearchResponse {\n    /// Array of search results (in input order).\n    pub results: Vec<SearchResult>,\n}\n\n/// Individual search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResult {\n    /// HTTP status code for this query.\n    pub status: u16,\n    /// Result body (if successful).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub body: Option<serde_json::Value>,\n    /// Error message (if failed).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    /// Error code (if failed).\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub code: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Search result data returned by the executor function.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResultData {\n    pub body: serde_json::Value,\n}\n\n/// Multi-search executor.\npub struct MultiSearchExecutor {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: MultiSearchConfig,\n}\n\nimpl MultiSearchExecutor {\n    /// Create a new multi-search executor.\n    pub fn new(config: MultiSearchConfig) -> Self {\n        Self { config }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new multi-search executor from advanced config.\n    pub fn from_advanced(config: AdvancedMultiSearchConfig) -> Self {\n        Self { config }\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a multi-search request.\n    ///\n    /// Executes each query independently and returns results in input order.\n    /// Each query is executed via the provided executor function.\n    pub async fn execute<F, Fut>(\n        &self,\n        request: MultiSearchRequest,\n        mut executor: F,\n    ) -> Result<MultiSearchResponse>\n    where\n        F: FnMut(SearchQuery) -> Fut,\n        Fut: std::future::Future<Output = Result<SearchResultData>>,\n    {\n        self.validate(&request)?;\n\n        // Execute all queries in parallel\n        let mut tasks = Vec::with_capacity(request.queries.len());\n        for query in request.queries {\n            tasks.push(executor(query));\n        }\n\n        let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n\n        // Convert results to SearchResults\n        let search_results: Vec<SearchResult> = results\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|r| match r {\n                Ok(data) => SearchResult {\n                    status: 200,\n                    body: Some(data.body),\n                    error: None,\n                    code: None,\n                },\n                Err(e) => SearchResult {\n                    status: 500,\n                    body: None,\n                    error: Some(e.to_string()),\n                    code: Some(\"internal_error\".to_string()),\n                },\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Ok(MultiSearchResponse {\n            results: search_results,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Validate a multi-search request.\n    pub fn validate(&self, request: &MultiSearchRequest) -> Result<()> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"multi-search is disabled\".into()));\n        }\n\n        if request.queries.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"queries array is empty\".into()));\n        }\n\n        if request.queries.len() > self.config.max_queries_per_batch as usize {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                \"too many queries: {} exceeds maximum of {}\",\n                request.queries.len(),\n                self.config.max_queries_per_batch\n            )));\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for MultiSearchExecutor {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(MultiSearchConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = MultiSearchConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_queries_per_batch, 100);\n        assert_eq!(config.total_timeout_ms, 30000);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_empty_queries() {\n        let executor = MultiSearchExecutor::default();\n        let request = MultiSearchRequest {\n            queries: vec![],\n        };\n        assert!(executor.validate(&request).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_too_many_queries() {\n        let mut config = MultiSearchConfig::default();\n        config.max_queries_per_batch = 10;\n        let executor = MultiSearchExecutor::new(config);\n\n        let queries: Vec<SearchQuery> = (0..20).map(|i| SearchQuery {\n            indexUid: format!(\"index-{}\", i),\n            q: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            filter: None,\n            limit: Some(10),\n            offset: Some(0),\n            other: HashMap::new(),\n        }).collect();\n\n        let request = MultiSearchRequest { queries };\n        assert!(executor.validate(&request).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_valid_request() {\n        let executor = MultiSearchExecutor::default();\n        let request = MultiSearchRequest {\n            queries: vec![SearchQuery {\n                indexUid: \"products\".into(),\n                q: Some(\"laptop\".into()),\n                filter: None,\n                limit: Some(20),\n                offset: Some(0),\n                other: HashMap::new(),\n            }],\n        };\n        assert!(executor.validate(&request).is_ok());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_query_serialization() {\n        let query = SearchQuery {\n            indexUid: \"products\".into(),\n            q: Some(\"laptop\".into()),\n            filter: Some(\"category = \\\"electronics\\\"\".into()),\n            limit: Some(20),\n            offset: Some(0),\n            other: HashMap::new(),\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&query).unwrap();\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"indexUid\\\":\\\"products\\\"\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\\\"laptop\\\"\"));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_multi_search() {\n        let executor = MultiSearchExecutor::default();\n\n        let request = MultiSearchRequest {\n            queries: vec![\n                SearchQuery {\n                    indexUid: \"products\".into(),\n                    q: Some(\"laptop\".into()),\n                    filter: None,\n                    limit: Some(20),\n                    offset: Some(0),\n                    other: HashMap::new(),\n                },\n                SearchQuery {\n                    indexUid: \"users\".into(),\n                    q: Some(\"john\".into()),\n                    filter: None,\n                    limit: Some(10),\n                    offset: Some(0),\n                    other: HashMap::new(),\n                },\n            ],\n        };\n\n        let response = executor\n            .execute(request, |query| async move {\n                Ok(SearchResultData {\n                    body: serde_json::json!({\n                        \"hits\": [],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n                        \"limit\": query.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n                        \"offset\": query.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 0,\n                    }),\n                })\n            })\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(response.results.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(response.results[0].status, 200);\n        assert!(response.results[0].body.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(response.results[1].status, 200);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":283,"startLine":1,"totalLines":283}}}

&PreflightRequest,\n383\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n384\t        let start = Instant::now();\n385\t        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n386\t\n387\t        tracing::debug!(\n388\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n389\t            node_id = %node,\n390\t            address = %address,\n391\t            index = %request.index_uid,\n392\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n393\t            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n394\t            \"node call started\"\n395\t        );\n396\t\n397\t        // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n398\t        let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n399\t        let stats_resp = self\n400\t            .client\n401\t            .get(&stats_url)\n402\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n403\t            .send()\n404\t            .await\n405\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n406\t\n407\t        if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n408\t            // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n409\t            return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n410\t                total_docs: 0,\n411\t                avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n412\t                term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n413\t            });\n414\t        }\n415\t\n416\t        let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n417\t            .json()\n418\t            .await\n419\t            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n420\t\n421\t        let total_docs = stats_body\n422\t            .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n423\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n424\t            .unwrap_or(0);\n425\t\n426\t        // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n427\t        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n428\t        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n429\t        for term in &request.terms {\n430\t            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n431\t\n432\t            let search_resp = self\n433\t                .client\n434\t                .post(&search_url)\n435\t                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n436\t                .json(&search_body)\n437\t                .send()\n438\t                .await\n439\t                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n440\t\n441\t            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n442\t                let body: Value = search_resp\n443\t                    .json()\n444\t                    .await\n445\t                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n446\t                let df = body\n447\t                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n448\t                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n449\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n450\t                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n451\t            }\n452\t        }\n453\t\n454\t        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n455\t        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n456\t        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n457\t\n458\t        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n459\t        tracing::debug!(\n460\t            target: \"miroir.node\",\n461\t            node_id = %node,\n462\t            address = %address,\n463\t            operation = \"preflight\",\n464\t            duration_ms,\n465\t            total_docs,\n466\t            \"node call completed\"\n467\t        );\n468\t\n469\t        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n470\t            total_docs,\n471\t            avg_doc_length,\n472\t            term_stats,\n473\t        })\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    fn get_task_status(\n477\t        &self,\n478\t        node: &NodeId,\n479\t        address: &str,\n480\t      );\n        let _guard = span.enter();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let url = self.search_url(address, &request.index_uid);\n\n        let mut body =     let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/documents/delete\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            request.index_uid\n        );\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n started\"\n        );\n\n        let response = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .json(&request.filter)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"delete_by_filter\",\n            duration_ms,\n            status = status.as_u16(),\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            // Try to parse as Meilisearch error\n            if let Ok(meili_err) = serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&body_text) {\n                return Ok(DeleteResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    task_uid: None,\n                    message: meili_err.get(\"message\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    code: meili_err.get(\"code\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                    error_type: meili_err.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                });\n            }\n            return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                status: status.as_u16(),\n                body: body_text,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Parse successful response\n        let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: json.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let base = address.trim_end_matches('/');\n\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            index = %request.index_uid,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            term_count = request.terms.len(),\n            \"node call started\"\n        );\n\n        // 1. Get total docs from Meilisearch stats endpoint\n        let stats_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", base, request.index_uid);\n        let stats_resp = self\n            .client\n            .get(&stats_url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Stats request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        if !stats_resp.status().is_success() {\n            // Index not found or node unreachable — return empty stats\n            return Ok(PreflightResponse {\n                total_docs: 0,\n                avg_doc_length: 0.0,\n                term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        let stats_body: Value = stats_resp\n            .json()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse stats: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_docs = stats_body\n            .get(\"numberOfDocuments\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // 2. Get DF for each term via search with limit=0\n        let mut term_stats = HashMap::new();\n        let search_url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/search\", base, request.index_uid);\n        for term in &request.terms {\n            let search_body = serde_json::json!({\"q\": term, \"limit\": 0});\n\n            let search_resp = self\n                .client\n                .post(&search_url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n                .json(&search_body)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"DF search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if search_resp.status().is_success() {\n                let body: Value = search_resp\n                    .json()\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse DF response: {}\", e)))?;\n                let df = body\n                    .get(\"estimatedTotalHits\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n                term_stats.insert(term.clone(), TermStats { df });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // 3. Estimate avg doc length (Meilisearch doesn't expose this directly;\n        //    use a default. The BM25 score is mainly sensitive to IDF, not avgdl.)\n        let avg_doc_length = 500.0;\n\n        let duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n        tracing::debug!(\n            target: \"miroir.node\",\n            node_id = %node,\n            address = %address,\n            operation = \"preflight\",\n            duration_ms,\n            total_docs,\n            \"node call completed\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs,\n            avg_doc_length,\n            term_stats,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let task_uid = request.task_uid;\n        let url = Self::task_url_static(address, task_uid);\n        let master_key = self.master_key.clone();\n        let client = self.client.clone();\n\n        async move {\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Request failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response\n                .text()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to read response: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                return Err(NodeError::HttpError {\n                    status: status.as_u16(),\n                    body: body_text,\n                });\n            }\n\n            // Parse successful response\n            let json: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n                NodeError::NetworkError(format!(\"Failed to parse JSON response: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: json.get(\"status\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"enqueued\")\n                    .to_string(),\n                error: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"message\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n                error_type: json.get(\"error\")\n                    .and_then(|v| v.get(\"type\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_url_construction() {\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\"test-key\".into(), 5000);\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.search_url(\"http://localhost:7700\", \"my_index\"),\n            \"http://localhost:7700/indexes/my_index/search\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            client.search_url(\"http://localhost:7700/\", \"my_index\"),\n            \"http://localhost:7700/indexes/my_index/search\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_preflight_url_construction() {\n        let client = HttpClient::new(\"test-key\".into(), 5000);\n RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n40\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n41\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n42\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner       if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n55\t            Some(Self(s))\n56\t        } else {\n57\t            None\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\tpub async fn reg!(peer_pod_count);\n757\t        reg!(leader);\n758\t        reg!(owned_shards_count);\n759\t\n760\t        // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n761\t        let Opts::new(\"miroir_admin_session_revoked_total\",\n767\t                \"Admin sessions revoked via logout\")\n768\t        ).expect(\"create admin_session_revoked_total\");\n769\t        reg!(admin_session_key_generated);\n770\t        reg!(admin_session_revoked_total);\n771\t\n772\t        Self {\n773\t            registry,\n774\t            request_duration,\n775\t
