crate::{\n    error::{MiroirError, Result},\n    task_store::{NewCanary, NewCanaryRun, CanaryRow, TaskStore},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Canary assertion types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryAssertion {\n    TopHitId { value: String },\n    TopKContains { k: usize, ids: Vec<String> },\n    MinHits { value: usize },\n    MaxP95Ms { value: u64 },\n    SettingsVersionAtLeast { value: i64 },\n    MustNotContainId { id: String },\n}\n\n/// Canary definition\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Canary {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n}\n\n/// Search query for canary\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchQuery {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Box<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Box<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Box<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>, max_concurrent: usize, run_history_limit: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, _index_uid: &str, _query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // TODO: Wire this to the actual search client\n        // For now, return a placeholder response\n        Ok(SearchResponse {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            estimated_total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            query: String::new(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n                // For now, this assertion always passes\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":108,"oldLines":12,"newStart":108,"newLines":22,"lines":[" }"," "," impl CanaryRunner {","-    pub fn new(store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>, max_concurrent: usize, §13.18 Synthetic canary queries with golden assertions\n\nuse crate::{\n    error::{MiroirError, Result},\n    task_store::{NewCanary, NewCanaryRun, CanaryRow, TaskStore},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Canary assertion types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryAssertion {\n    TopHitId { value: String },\n    TopKContains { k: usize, ids: Vec<String> },\n    MinHits { value: usize },\n    MaxP95Ms { value: u64 },\n    SettingsVersionAtLeast { value: i64 },\n    MustNotContainId { id: String },\n}\n\n/// Canary definition\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Canary {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n}\n\n/// Search query for canary\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchQuery {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Box<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Box<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Box<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        max_concurrent: usize,\n        run_history_limit: usize,\n        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n        metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n        settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n            search_executor,\n            metrics_emitter,\n            settings_version_checker,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, _index_uid: &str, _query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // TODO: Wire this to the actual search client\n        // For now, return a placeholder response\n        Ok(SearchResponse {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            estimated_total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            query: String::new(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n                // For now, this assertion always passes\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryAssertion {\n    TopHitId { value: String },\n    TopKContains { k: usize, ids: Vec<String> },\n    MinHits { value: usize },\n    MaxP95Ms { value: u64 },\n    SettingsVersionAtLeast { value: i64 },\n    MustNotContainId { id: String },\n}\n\n/// Canary definition\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Canary {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n}\n\n/// Search query for canary\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchQuery {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Box<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Box<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Box<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        max_concurrent: usize,\n        run_history_limit: usize,\n        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n        metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n        settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n            search_executor,\n            metrics_emitter,\n            settings_version_checker,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, _index_uid: &str, _query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // TODO: Wire this to the actual search client\n        // For now, return a placeholder response\n        Ok(SearchResponse {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            estimated_total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            query: String::new(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n                // For now, this assertion always passes\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n            search_executor: Box::new({\n                let executor = &self.search_executor;\n                move |index, query| executor(index, query)\n            }),\n            metrics_emitter: Box::new({\n                let emitter = &self.metrics_emitter;\n                move |result| emitter(result)\n            }),\n            settings_version_checker: Box::new({\n                let checker = &self.settings_version_checker;\n                move |index| checker(index)\n            }),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":267,"oldLines":15,"newStart":267,"newLines":9,"lines":["     }"," ","     /// Execute a search query against the golden assertions\n\nuse crate::{\n    error::{MiroirError, Result},\n    task_store::{NewCanary, NewCanaryRun, CanaryRow, TaskStore},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Canary assertion types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryAssertion {\n    TopHitId { value: String },\n    TopKContains { k: usize, ids: Vec<String> },\n    MinHits { value: usize },\n    MaxP95Ms { value: u64 },\n    SettingsVersionAtLeast { value: i64 },\n    MustNotContainId { id: String },\n}\n\n/// Canary definition\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Canary {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n}\n\n/// Search query for canary\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchQuery {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Box<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Box<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Box<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        max_concurrent: usize,\n        run_history_limit: usize,\n        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n        metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n        settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n            search_executor,\n            metrics_emitter,\n            settings_version_checker,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // Call the search executor callback\n        (self.search_executor)(index_uid, query)\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // TODO: Get current settings version from the store\n                // For now, this assertion always passes\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n            search_executor: Box::new({\n                let executor = &self.search_executor;\n                move |index, query| executor(index, query)\n            }),\n            metrics_emitter: Box::new({\n                let emitter = &self.metrics_emitter;\n                move |result| emitter(result)\n            }),\n            settings_version_checker: Box::new({\n                let checker = &self.settings_version_checker;\n                move |index| checker(index)\n            }),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":336,"oldLines":8,"newStart":336,"newLines":18,"lines":["                 }","             }"," Result<()> {\n144\t        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n145\t        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n146\t\n147\t        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n148\t        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n149\t        for canary in canaries {\n150\t            if !canary.enabled {\n151\t                continue;\n152\t            }\n153\t\n154\t            // Check if already running\n155\t            let running = self.running.read().await;\n156\t            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n157\t                continue;\n158\t            }\n159\t            drop(running);\n160\t\n161\t            // Check last run time\n162\t            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n163\t } else {\n167\t                true\n168\t            };\n169\t\n170\t            if should_run {\n171\t                due_canaries.push(canary);\n172\t            }\n173\t        }\n174\t\n175\t runner = self.clone_runner();\n179\t\n180\t            // Mark as running\n181\t            {\n182\t                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n183\t                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n184\t            }\n185\t\n186\t            tokio::spawn(async move {\n187\t                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n188\t\n189\t                // Mark as not running\n190\t                {\n191\t                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n192\t                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n193\t failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n233\t            CanaryStatus::Passed\n234\t        } else {\n235\t            CanaryStatus::Failed\n236\t        };\n237\t\n238\t        let result = CanaryRunResult };\n247\t\n248\t        // Store the run\n249\t        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n250\t            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n251\t            ran_at: now,\n252\t            status:             None\n256\t            } else {\n257\t                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n258\t            },\n259\t        };\n260\t\n261\t        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n262\t\n263\t        // Emit metrics\n264\t        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n265\t\n266\t        Ok(result)\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    /// Execute a search query against the index\n270\t    async fn execute_search(&self, index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n271\t        // Call the search executor callback\n272\t        (self.search_executor)(index_uid, query)\n273\t    }\n274\t\n275\t    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n276\t    fn evaluate_assertion(\n277\t        &self,\n278\t        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n279\t        response: &SearchResponse,\n280\t        latency_ms: i64,\n281\t        index_uid: &str,\n282\t    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure>              actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n295\t                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n296\t                    });\n297\t                }\n298\t            }\n299\t            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n300\t                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n301\t                    .take(*k)\n302\t                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n303\t                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n304\t                    .collect();\n305\t\n306\t                let        });\n317\t                }\n318\t            }\n319\t            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n320\t                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n321\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n322\t                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n323\t                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n324\t                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n325\t                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n326\t                    });\n327\t                }\n328\t            }\n329\t            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n330\t                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n331\t                    return actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n335\t                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n336\t                    });\n337\t                }\n338\t            }\n339\t            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n340\t                // Get current settings version for the index\n341\t                let current_version = (self.settings_version_checker)(index_uid)\n342\t                    .unwrap_or(0);\n343\t\n344\t                if current_version < *value {\n345\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n346\t                        assertion_type: \"settings_version_at_least\".to_string(),\n347\t                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n348\t                        actual: serde_json::json!(current_version),\n349\t                        message: format!(\"Settings version below minimum: {} < {}\", current_version, value),\n350\t .unwrap_or(false));\n359\t\n360\t                if contains {\n361\t                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n362\t                        assertion_type:
