Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Arc<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Arc<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Arc<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        max_concurrent: usize,\n        run_history_limit: usize,\n        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n        metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n        settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n            search_executor,\n            metrics_emitter,\n            settings_version_checker,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms, &canary.index_uid) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // Call the search executor callback\n        (self.search_executor)(index_uid, query)\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n        index_uid: &str,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // Get current settings version for the index\n                let current_version = (self.settings_version_checker)(index_uid)\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n\n                if current_version < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"settings_version_at_least\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(current_version),\n                        message: format!(\"Settings version below minimum: {} < {}\", current_version, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n            search_executor: self.search_executor.clone(),\n            metrics_emitter: self.metrics_emitter.clone(),\n            settings_version_checker: self.settings_version_checker.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n    }\n}\n","numLines":492,"startLine":1,"totalLines":492}}}

       canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }","originalFile":"//! §13.18 Synthetic canary queries with golden assertions\n\nuse crate::{\n    error::{MiroirError, Result},\n    task_store::{NewCanary, NewCanaryRun, CanaryRow, TaskStore},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Canary assertion types\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryAssertion {\n    TopHitId { value: String },\n    TopKContains { k: usize, ids: Vec<String> },\n    MinHits { value: usize },\n    MaxP95Ms { value: u64 },\n    SettingsVersionAtLeast { value: i64 },\n    MustNotContainId { id: String },\n}\n\n/// Canary definition\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Canary {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n}\n\n/// Search query for canary\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchQuery {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub params: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run result\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunResult {\n    pub canary_id: String,\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: CanaryStatus,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: Vec<AssertionFailure>,\n    pub hit_count: usize,\n    pub top_hit_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Canary run status\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum CanaryStatus {\n    Passed,\n    Failed,\n    Error,\n}\n\n/// Assertion failure details\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssertionFailure {\n    pub assertion_type: String,\n    pub expected: serde_json::Value,\n    pub actual: serde_json::Value,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Search response from Meilisearch\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SearchResponse {\n    pub hits: Vec<Hit>,\n    pub estimated_total_hits: usize,\n    pub processing_time_ms: usize,\n    pub query: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Hit {\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub fields: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Search executor callback for canary queries.\npub type SearchExecutor = Arc<dyn Fn(&str, &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Metrics emitter callback for canary runs.\npub type MetricsEmitter = Arc<dyn Fn(&CanaryRunResult) + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Settings version checker callback.\npub type SettingsVersionChecker = Arc<dyn Fn(&str) -> Option<i64> + Send + Sync>;\n\n/// Canary runner\npub struct CanaryRunner {\n    store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    running: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, bool>>>,\n    max_concurrent: usize,\n    run_history_limit: usize,\n    search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n    metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n    settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n}\n\nimpl CanaryRunner {\n    pub fn new(\n        store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        max_concurrent: usize,\n        run_history_limit: usize,\n        search_executor: SearchExecutor,\n        metrics_emitter: MetricsEmitter,\n        settings_version_checker: SettingsVersionChecker,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store,\n            running: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit,\n            search_executor,\n            metrics_emitter,\n            settings_version_checker,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background canary runner\n    pub async fn start(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(5));\n\n        loop {\n            interval.tick().await;\n            if let Err(e) = self.run_due_canaries().await {\n                tracing::error!(\"Canary runner error: {}\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run all canaries that are due\n    async fn run_due_canaries(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let canaries = self.store.list_canaries()?;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Filter enabled canaries that are due\n        let mut due_canaries = Vec::new();\n        for canary in canaries {\n            if !canary.enabled {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if already running\n            let running = self.running.read().await;\n            if running.get(&canary.id).copied().unwrap_or(false) {\n                continue;\n            }\n            drop(running);\n\n            // Check last run time\n            let runs = self.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1)?;\n            let should_run = if let Some(last_run) = runs.first() {\n                let elapsed_ms = now - last_run.ran_at;\n                elapsed_ms >= (canary.interval_s * 1000)\n            } else {\n                true\n            };\n\n            if should_run {\n                due_canaries.push(canary);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Run up to max_concurrent canaries\n        for canary in due_canaries.into_iter().take(self.max_concurrent) {\n            let canary_id = canary.id.clone();\n            let runner = self.clone_runner();\n\n            // Mark as running\n            {\n                let mut running = self.running.write().await;\n                running.insert(canary_id.clone(), true);\n            }\n\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                let result = runner.run_canary(&canary).await;\n\n                // Mark as not running\n                {\n                    let mut running = runner.running.write().await;\n                    running.remove(&canary_id);\n                }\n\n                if let Err(e) = result {\n                    tracing::error!(\"Canary {} failed: {}\", canary_id, e);\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Run a single canary\n    async fn run_canary(&self, canary: &CanaryRow) -> Result<CanaryRunResult> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        // Parse query\n        let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_str(&canary.query_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary query: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Parse assertions\n        let assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion> = serde_json::from_str(&canary.assertions_json)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Invalid canary assertions: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Execute the search query against the index\n        // Note: This would need to be wired to the actual search client\n        // For now, we simulate a search response\n        let search_response = self.execute_search(&canary.index_uid, &query).await?;\n\n        let latency_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as i64;\n\n        // Evaluate assertions\n        let mut failed_assertions = Vec::new();\n        for assertion in &assertions {\n            if let Some(failure) = self.evaluate_assertion(&assertion, &search_response, latency_ms, &canary.index_uid) {\n                failed_assertions.push(failure);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let status = if failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed\n        } else {\n            CanaryStatus::Failed\n        };\n\n        let result = CanaryRunResult {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status,\n            latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions,\n            hit_count: search_response.hits.len(),\n            top_hit_id: search_response.hits.first().and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(|s| s.to_string())),\n        };\n\n        // Store the run\n        let new_run = NewCanaryRun {\n            canary_id: canary.id.clone(),\n            ran_at: now,\n            status: serde_json::to_string(&result.status).unwrap_or_default(),\n            latency_ms: result.latency_ms,\n            failed_assertions_json: if result.failed_assertions.is_empty() {\n                None\n            } else {\n                Some(serde_json::to_string(&result.failed_assertions).unwrap_or_default())\n            },\n        };\n\n        self.store.insert_canary_run(&new_run, self.run_history_limit)?;\n\n        // Emit metrics\n        self.emit_metrics(&result);\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a search query against the index\n    async fn execute_search(&self, index_uid: &str, query: &SearchQuery) -> Result<SearchResponse> {\n        // Call the search executor callback\n        (self.search_executor)(index_uid, query)\n    }\n\n    /// Evaluate a single assertion\n    fn evaluate_assertion(\n        &self,\n        assertion: &CanaryAssertion,\n        response: &SearchResponse,\n        latency_ms: i64,\n        index_uid: &str,\n    ) -> Option<AssertionFailure> {\n        match assertion {\n            CanaryAssertion::TopHitId { value } => {\n                let top_hit_id = response.hits.first()\n                    .and_then(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n                let actual = top_hit_id.unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if actual != value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_hit_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(actual),\n                        message: format!(\"Top hit ID mismatch: expected {}, got {}\", value, actual),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::TopKContains { k, ids } => {\n                let top_k_ids: Vec<&str> = response.hits.iter()\n                    .take(*k)\n                    .filter_map(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\"))\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n                    .collect();\n\n                let missing: Vec<_> = ids.iter()\n                    .filter(|id| !top_k_ids.contains(&id.as_str()))\n                    .collect();\n\n                if !missing.is_empty() {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"top_k_contains\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(ids),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(top_k_ids),\n                        message: format!(\"Top {} missing IDs: {:?}\", k, missing),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value } => {\n                if response.hits.len() < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"min_hits\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(response.hits.len()),\n                        message: format!(\"Hit count below minimum: {} < {}\", response.hits.len(), value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value } => {\n                if latency_ms as u64 > *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"max_p95_ms\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(latency_ms),\n                        message: format!(\"Latency exceeded p95: {}ms > {}ms\", latency_ms, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::SettingsVersionAtLeast { value } => {\n                // Get current settings version for the index\n                let current_version = (self.settings_version_checker)(index_uid)\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n\n                if current_version < *value {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"settings_version_at_least\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(value),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(current_version),\n                        message: format!(\"Settings version below minimum: {} < {}\", current_version, value),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n            CanaryAssertion::MustNotContainId { id } => {\n                let contains = response.hits.iter()\n                    .any(|h| h.fields.get(\"id\")\n                        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                        .map(|v| v == id.as_str())\n                        .unwrap_or(false));\n\n                if contains {\n                    return Some(AssertionFailure {\n                        assertion_type: \"must_not_contain_id\".to_string(),\n                        expected: serde_json::json!(null),\n                        actual: serde_json::json!(id),\n                        message: format!(\"Results contain forbidden ID: {}\", id),\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Emit metrics for a canary run\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, result: &CanaryRunResult) {\n        // TODO: Wire to prometheus metrics\n        // For now, just log\n        match result.status {\n            CanaryStatus::Passed => {\n                tracing::info!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    \"Canary passed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Failed => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    latency_ms = result.latency_ms,\n                    failures = result.failed_assertions.len(),\n                    \"Canary failed\"\n                );\n            }\n            CanaryStatus::Error => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    canary = %result.canary_id,\n                    \"Canary error\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the runner for use in spawned tasks\n    fn clone_runner(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            store: self.store.clone(),\n            running: self.running.clone(),\n            max_concurrent: self.max_concurrent,\n            run_history_limit: self.run_history_limit,\n            search_executor: self.search_executor.clone(),\n            metrics_emitter: self.metrics_emitter.clone(),\n            settings_version_checker: self.settings_version_checker.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a definition\npub fn create_canary(\n    id: String,\n    name: String,\n    index_uid: String,\n    interval_s: i64,\n    query: SearchQuery,\n    assertions: Vec<CanaryAssertion>,\n) -> Result<NewCanary> {\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    Ok(NewCanary {\n        id: id.clone(),\n        name,\n        index_uid,\n        interval_s,\n        query_json: serde_json::to_string(&query)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize query: {}\", e)))?,\n        assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"Failed to serialize assertions: {}\", e)))?,\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Capture a query from traffic as a canary candidate\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct CapturedQuery {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: SearchQuery,\n    pub response: SearchResponse,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\npub struct QueryCapture {\n    queries: Arc<RwLock<Vec<CapturedQuery>>>,\n    max_queries: usize,\n}\n\nimpl QueryCapture {\n    pub fn new(max_queries: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            queries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n            max_queries,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Capture a query for canary creation\n    pub async fn capture(&self, index_uid: String, query: SearchQuery, response: SearchResponse) {\n        let mut queries = self.queries.write().await;\n        let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n        queries.push(CapturedQuery {\n            index_uid,\n            query,\n            response,\n            timestamp: now,\n        });\n\n        // Keep only the most recent queries\n        let len = queries.len();\n        if len > self.max_queries {\n            queries.drain(0..len - self.max_queries);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get captured queries\n    pub async fn get_captured(&self) -> Vec<CapturedQuery> {\n        self.queries.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear captured queries\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        self.queries.write().await.clear();\n scoped_key_rotation::ScopedKeyRotationState;\n31\t\n32\t/// Unified application state containing all shared state.\n33\t#[derive(Clone)]\n34\tstruct UnifiedState {\n35\t    auth: AuthState,\n36\t    metrics: Metrics,\n37\t    admin: admin_endpoints::AppState,\n38\t    pod_id: String,\n39\t    redis_store: Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\timpl UnifiedState {\n43\t    fn new(config: MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n44\t        let metrics = Metrics::new(&config);\n45\t\n46\t        let master_key = std::env::var(\"MIROIR_MASTER_KEY\")\n47\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| config.master_key.clone());\n48\t\n49\t        let admin_key = std::env::var(\"MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY\")\n50\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| config.admin.api_key.clone());\n51\t\n52\t        let jwt_primary = if config.search_ui.enabled {\n53\t            std::env::var(&config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env).ok()\n54\t        } else {\n55\t            None\n56\t        };\n57\t\n58\t        let jwt_previous = std::env::var(&config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_previous_env)\n59\t            .ok()\n60\t            .filter(|v| !v.is_empty());\n61\t\n62\t        let seal_key = SealKey::from_env_or_generate();\n63\t\n64\t        // Set the key-generated gauge before constructing AuthState\n65\t        // so the metric is accurate from the first scrape.\n66\t        metrics.admin_session_key_generated().set(if seal_key.is_generated() { 1.0 } else { 0.0 });\n67\t\n68\t        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n69\t\n70\t        // Create Redis task store if backend is redis (must happen before AppState\n71\t        // so redis_store and pod_id are available to admin endpoints).\n72\t        let redis_store = if config.task_store.backend == \"redis\" && !config.task_store.url.is_empty() {\n73\t            let url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n74\t            Some(\n75\t                tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n76\t                    tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(\n77\t                        miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(&url)\n78\t                    )\n79\t                })\n80\t                .expect(\"Failed to connect to Redis for scoped key rotation\"),\n81\t            )\n82\t        } else {\n83\t            None\n84\t        };\n85\t\n86\t        let auth = AuthState {\n87\t            master_key,\n88\t            admin_key: admin_key.clone(),\n89\t            jwt_primary,\n90\t            jwt_previous,\n91\t            seal_key: seal_key.clone(),\n92\t            revoked_sessions: std::sync::Arc::new(dashmap::DashMap::new()),\n93\t            admin_session_revoked_total: metrics.admin_session_revoked_total(),\n94\t        };\n95\t\n96\t        let admin = admin_endpoints::AppState::with_redis(\n97\t            config.clone(),\n98\t            metrics.clone(),\n99\t            redis_store.clone(),\n100\t            pod_id.clone(),\n101\t            seal_key.clone(),\n102\t        );\n103\t\n104\t        Self { auth, metrics, admin, pod_id, redis_store }\n105\t    }\n106\t}\n107\t\n108\t// Implement FromRef so that admin_endpoints::AppState can be extracted from UnifiedState\n109\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for admin_endpoints::AppState {\n110\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n111\t        Self {\n112\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n113\t            topology: state.admin.topology.clone(),\n114\t            ready: state.admin.ready.clone(),\n115\t            metrics: state.admin.metrics.clone(),\n116\t            version_state: local_search_ui_rate_limiter: admin_endpoints::LocalSearchUiRateLimiter::new(),\n123\t            rebalancer: state.admin.rebalancer.clone(),\n124\t            migration_coordinator: state.admin.migration_coordinator.clone(),\n125\t        }\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t// Implement FromRef so that TelemetryState can be extracted from UnifiedState\n130\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for TelemetryState {\n131\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n132\t        TelemetryState {\n133\t            metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n134\t            pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n135\t        }\n136\t    }\n137\t}\n138\t\n139\t// Implement FromRef so that CsrfState can be extracted from UnifiedState\n140\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for for routes::aliases::AliasState {\n151\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n152\t        Self {\n153\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n154\t            task_registry: FromRef<UnifiedState> for routes::explain::ExplainState {\n161\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n162\t        Self {\n163\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n164\t            topology: state.admin.topology.clone(),\n165\t        }\n166\t    }\n167\t}\n168\t\n169\t// Implement FromRef so that routes::multi_search::MultiSearchState can be extracted from UnifiedState\n170\timpl FromRef<UnifiedState> for routes::multi_search::MultiSearchState {\n171\t    fn from_ref(state: &UnifiedState) -> Self {\n172\t        Self {\n173\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n174\t            topology: state.admin.topology.clone(),\n175\t            node_master_key: state.admin.config.master_key.clone(),\n176\t            metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n177\t        }\n178\t    }\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t#[tokio::main]\n182\tasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n183\t    // Load configuration (file → env → CLI overlay)\n184\t    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n185\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", e))?;\n186\t\n187\t    // Initialize structured JSON logging (plan §10 format)\n188\t    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, message, pod_id\n189\t    // Per-request fields (request_id) are added by telemetry middleware span.\n190\t    let filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()\n191\t        .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new(\"info\"));\n192\t\n193\t    let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n194\t\n195\t    // Build subscriber - conditionally add OTel layer\n196\t    // Note: We rebuild the layers in each branch because the types differ\n197\t    // OTel layer must be applied to the bare registry first\n198\t    if let Some(otel_layer) = otel::init_otel_layer(&config) {\n199\t        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n200\t            .json()\n201\t            .flatten_event(true)\n202\t            .with_target(true)\n203\t            .with_current_span(true)\n204\t            .with_span_list(false);\n205\t        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n206\t        registry()\n207\t            .with(otel_layer)\n208\t            .with(filter)\n209\t            .with(json_layer)\n210\t            .init();\n211\t    } else {\n212\t        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n213\t            .json()\n214\t            .flatten_event(true)\n215\t            .with_target(true)\n216\t            .with_current_span(true)\n217\t            .with_span_list(false);\n218\t        registry()\n219\t            .with(filter)\n220\t            .with(json_layer)\n221\t            .init();\n222\t    }\n223\t\n224\t    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n225\t    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n226\t    // left — its fields propagate to all child spans and events.\n227\t    let _pod_span = tracing::info_span!(\"runtime\", pod_id = %pod_id).entered();\n228\t\n229\t    info!(\n230\t        shards = config.shards,\n231\t        replication_factor = config.replication_factor,\n232\t        replica_groups = config.replica_groups,\n233\t        \"miroir-proxy starting\"\n234\t    );\n235\t\n236\t    // Validate critical secrets at startup (plan §9: \"orchestrator refuses to\n237\t    // start the search UI without it\").\n238\t    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n239\t        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n240\t        match std::env::var(jwt_env) {\n241\t            Ok(v) if !v.is_empty() => {}\n242\t            _ => {\n243\t                anyhow::bail!(\n244\t                    \"search_ui is enabled but {} is not set — refusing to start. \\\n245\t                     Either set the env var or disable search_ui (search_ui.enabled: false)\",\n246\t                    jwt_env\n247\t                );\n248\t            }\n249\t        }\n250\t    }\n251\t\n252\t    // Build unified state\n253\t    let state = UnifiedState::new(config.clone());\n254\t\n255\t    // Start health checker background task\n256\t    let health_checker_state = state.admin.clone();\n257\t    tokio::spawn(async move {\n258\t        run_health_checker(health_checker_state).await;\n259\t    });\n260\t\n261\t    // Start scoped key rotation background task (requires Redis)\n262\t    if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n263\t        let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n264\t            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n265\t            redis: redis.clone(),\n266\t            pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n267\t        };\n268\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n269\t            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n270\t        });\n271\t\n272\t        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n273\t        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n274\t        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n275\t        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n276\t        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n277\t        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n278\t        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n279\t        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n280\t        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n281\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n282\t            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n283\t            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n284\t                &redis_url,\n285\t                &key_prefix,\n286\t                move |session_id: String| {\n287\t                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n288\t                    revoked_total.inc();\n289\t                },\n290\t            )\n291\t            .await\n292\t            {\n293\t                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n294\t            }\n295\t        });\n296\t    }\n297\t\n298\t    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n299\t    let app = Router::new()\n300\t        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n301\t        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n302\t        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n303\t        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n304\t        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n305\t        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n306\t        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n307\t        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n308\t        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n309\t        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n310\t        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n311\t        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n312\t        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n313\t        // 2. auth_middleware\n314\t        // 3. Extension layers\n315\t        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n316\t        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n317\t        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n318\t        //\n319\t        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n320\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n321\t            TelemetryState {\n322\t                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n323\t                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n324\t            },\n325\t            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n326\t        ))\n327\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n328\t            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n329\t        ))\n330\t        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n331\t            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n332\t        ))\n333\t        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n334\t        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n335\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n336\t            state.auth.clone(),\n337\t            auth::auth_middleware,\n338\t        ))\n339\t        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n340\t            auth::CsrfState {\n341\t                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n342\t                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n343\t            },\n344\t            auth::csrf_middleware,\n345\t        ))\n346\t        .with_state(state.clone());\n347\t\n348\t    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n349\t        .parse()\n350\t        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n351\t    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n352\t\n353\t    info!(\n354\t        main_addr = %main_addr,\n355\t        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n356\t        \"listening\"\n357\t    );\n358\t\n359\t    // Create listeners\n360\t    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n361\t    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n362\t\n363\t    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n364\t    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n365\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n366\t\n367\t    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n368\t    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n369\t    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n370\t        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n371\t\n372\t    // Run both servers concurrently\n373\t    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n374\t        main_server,\n375\t        metrics_server\n376\t    );\n377\t\n378\t    // Check for errors\n379\t    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n380\t        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n381\t    }\n382\t    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n383\t        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n384\t    }\n385\t\n386\t    Ok(())\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n390\t///\n391\t/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n392\t/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\n393\tasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n394\t    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n395\t        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n396\t    ));\n397\t\n398\t    loop {\n399\t        interval.tick().await;\n400\t\n401\t        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n402\t        let mut all_healthy = true;\n403\t\n404\t        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n405\t        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n406\t\n407\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n408\t            // Get current node status\n409\t            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n410\t\n411\t            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n412\t            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n413\t                continue;\n414\t            }\n415\t\n416\t            // Get node address\n417\t            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n418\t                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n419\t                None => {\n420\t                    all_healthy = false;\n421\t                    continue;\n422\t                }\n423\t            };\n424\t\n425\t            // Try to reach the node\n426\t            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n427\t                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n428\t                .build()\n429\t            {\n430\t                Ok(c) => c,\n431\t                Err(_) => {\n432\t                    all_healthy = false;\n433\t                    continue;\n434\t                }\n435\t            };\n436\t\n437\t            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n438\t            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n439\t\n440\t            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n441\t                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n442\t                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n443\t                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n444\t                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n445\t                }\n446\t            } else {\n447\t                all_healthy = false;\n448\t            }\n449\t        }\n450\t\n451\t        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n452\t        for node_id in &node_ids {\n453\t            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n454\t            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n455\t        }\n456\t\n457\t        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n458\t        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n459\t\n460\t        // Update task registry size gauge\n461\t        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n462\t        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n463\t\n464\t        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n465\t        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n466\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n467\t        }\n468\t\n469\t        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n470\t        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n471\t    }\n472\t}\n473\t\n474\t/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n475\t/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\n476\tfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n477\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n478\t    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n479\t    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n480\t\n481\t    // Per-node shard count\n482\t    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n483\t        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n484\t\n485\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n486\t        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n487\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n488\t            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n489\t                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n490\t            );\n491\t            for node_id in &assigned {\n492\t                let healthy = node_map\n493\t                    .get(node_id)\n494\t                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n495\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n496\t                if healthy {\n497\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n498\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n499\t                }\n500\t            }\n501\t        }\n502\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n503\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n504\t        } else {\n505\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n506\t        }\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n510\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n511\t    } else {\n512\t        1.0\n513\t    };\n514\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n515\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n516\t\n517\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n518\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n519\t    }\n520\t}\n521\t\n522\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n523\t///\n524\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n525\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n526\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n527\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n528\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n529\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n530\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n531\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n532\t\n533\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n534\t        if max > 0 {\n535\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n536\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n537\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n538\t        }\n539\t    }\n540\t\n541\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n542\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n543\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n544\t        for line in contents.lines() {\n545\t            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n546\t                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n547\t                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n548\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n549\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n550\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n551\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n552\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n553\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n554\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n555\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n556\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n557\t                }\n558\t            }\n559\t        }\n560\t    }\n561\t\n562\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n563\t    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n564\t    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n565\t    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n566\t    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n567\t    metrics.set_leader(true);\n568\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n569\t}\n570\t\n571\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n572\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n573\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n574\t        .ok()\n575\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n576\t}\n577\t\n578\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n579\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n580\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n581\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n582\t            .await\n583\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n584\t    };\n585\t\n586\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n587\t    let terminate = async {\n588\t        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n589\t            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n590\t            .recv()\n591\t            .await;\n592\t    };\n593\t\n594\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n595\t    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n596\t\n597\t    tokio::select! {\n598\t        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n599\t        _ = terminate => {},\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n603\t\n604\t    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n605\t    otel::shutdown_otel();\n606\t}\n607\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c278b2b0-d41b-493d-8c12-dc91cdad82aa","uuid":"68a0e84c-167e-473a-a142-ed6c0f28704c","timestamp":"2026-05-03T22:51:25.954Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/main.rs","content":"use env → CLI overlay)\n    let config = MiroirConfig::load()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Failed to load config: {}\", e))?;\n\n    // Initialize structured JSON logging (plan §10 format)\n    // Fields on every line: timestamp, level, target, message, pod_id\n    // Per-request fields (request_id) are added by telemetry middleware span.\n    let filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()\n        .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new(\"info\"));\n\n    let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n\n    // Build subscriber - conditionally add OTel layer\n    // Note: We rebuild the layers in each branch because the types differ\n    // OTel layer must be applied to the bare registry first\n    if let Some(otel_layer) = otel::init_otel_layer(&config) {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n        registry()\n            .with(otel_layer)\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    } else {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        registry()\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    }\n\n    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n    // left — its fields config.replication_factor,\n        replica_groups = config.replica_groups,\n        \"miroir-proxy starting\"\n    );\n\n    // Validate critical secrets at startup (plan §9: \"orchestrator refuses to\n    // start the search UI without it\").\n    if config.search_ui.enabled {\n        let jwt_env = &config.search_ui.auth.jwt_secret_env;\n        match std::env::var(jwt_env) {\n            Ok(v) if !v.is_empty() => {}\n            _ => {\n                anyhow::bail!(\n                    \"search_ui is enabled but {} is not set — refusing to start. \\\n                     Either set the env var or disable search_ui (search_ui.enabled: false)\",\n                    jwt_env\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build unified state\n    let state = run_health_checker(health_checker_state).await;\n    });\n\n    // Start scoped key rotation background task (requires Redis)\n    if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n            config: state.admin.config.clone(),\n            redis: redis.clone(),\n            pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n        };\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n        });\n\n        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n                &redis_url,\n                &key_prefix,\n                move |session_id: String| {\n                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n                    revoked_total.inc();\n                },\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        //\n        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n    metrics.set_leader(true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":607,"startLine":1,"totalLines":607}}}

Canary routes\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRef, Path, State},\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Json,\n    routing::{delete, get, post, put},\n    Router,\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    canary::{Canary, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Canary management state.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct CanaryState {\n    pub store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    pub capture: Arc<QueryCapture>,\n}\n\n/// Canary creation request\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct CreateCanaryRequest {\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    pub query: serde_json::Value,\n    pub assertions: Vec<serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Canary status response\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CanaryStatusResponse {\n    pub canaries: Vec<CanaryInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CanaryInfo {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub name: String,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub interval_s: i64,\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub created_at: i64,\n    pub last_run: Option<CanaryRunInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CanaryRunInfo {\n    pub ran_at: i64,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub latency_ms: i64,\n    pub failed_assertions: usize,\n}\n\n/// Capture request\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct CaptureRequest {\n    pub max_queries: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n/// Capture response\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CaptureResponse {\n    pub capture_id: String,\n    pub queries: Vec<CapturedQueryInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct CapturedQueryInfo {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: serde_json::Value,\n    pub timestamp: i64,\n}\n\n/// Create a new canary\npub async fn create_canary<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Json(req): Json<CreateCanaryRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n    let now = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp_millis();\n\n    // Parse query\n    let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_value(serde_json::json!(req.query))\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Invalid canary query\");\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        })?;\n\n    // Parse assertions\n    let assertions: tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Invalid canary assertion\");\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        })?;\n\n    // Create NewCanary for storage\n    let new_canary = NewCanary {\n        id:      assertions_json: serde_json::to_string(&assertions).unwrap_or_default(),\n        enabled: true,\n        created_at: now,\n    };\n\n    state.store.upsert_canary(&new_canary).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to store canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": id,\n        \"status\": \"created\"\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Get canary status\npub async fn get_canary_status<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n) -> Result<Json<CanaryStatusResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let canaries = state.store.list_canaries().map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to list canaries\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    let canary_infos: Vec<CanaryInfo> = canaries\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|canary| {\n            let runs = state.store.get_canary_runs(&canary.id, 1).unwrap_or_default();\n            let last_run = runs.first().map(|r| CanaryRunInfo {\n                ran_at: r.ran_at,\n                status: r.status.clone(),\n                latency_ms: r.latency_ms,\n                failed_assertions: r.failed_assertions_json.as_ref().map(|j| {\n canary.created_at,\n                last_run,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(Json(CanaryStatusResponse {\n        canaries: canary_infos,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// Get a specific canary\npub async fn get_canary<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Path(id): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    // Verify canary exists\n    let _existing = state.store.get_canary(&id).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to get canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?\n    .ok_or_else(|| StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    // Parse query\n    let query: SearchQuery = serde_json::from_value(serde_json::json!(req.query))\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Invalid canary query\");\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n _existing.created_at,\n    };\n\n    state.store.upsert_canary(&new_canary).map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"Failed to store canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n Ok(StatusCode::NO_CONTENT)\n}\n\n/// Start capturing queries for canary creation\npub async fn start_capture<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Json(req): Json<CaptureRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<CaptureResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let capture_id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n    let _max_queries = req.max_queries.unwrap_or(100);\n\n    // Clear previous captures\n    state.capture.clear().await;\n\n    Ok(Json(CaptureResponse {\n        capture_id,\n        queries: Vec::new(),\n    }))\n}\n\n/// Get captured queries\npub async fn get_captured<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let queries = state.capture.get_captured().await;\n\n    let query_infos: Vec<CapturedQueryInfo> = queries\n        .iter()\n        .map(|q| CapturedQueryInfo {\n            index_uid: q.index_uid.clone(),\n            query: serde_json::to_value(&q.query).unwrap_or_default(),\n            timestamp: q.timestamp,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"queries\": query_infos\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Create a canary from a captured query\npub async fn create_from_capture<S>(\n    State(state): State<CanaryState>,\n    Path(index_uid): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let queries = state.capture.get_captured().await;\n\n    // Find the first captured query matching the index UID\n    let captured = queries\n        .iter()\n        .find(|q| q.index_uid == index_uid)\n        .ok_or_else(|| StatusCode::NOT_FOUND)?;\n\n    let id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n\n    // Create a default canary from the captured query\n    let canary = miroir_core::canary::create_canary(\n        id.clone(),\n        format!(\"canary-{}\", captured.index_uid),\n        captured.index_uid.clone(),\n        3600, // Default: run every hour\n        captured.query.clone(),\n        vec![\n            CanaryAssertion::MinHits { value: 1 },\n            CanaryAssertion::MaxP95Ms { value: 500 },\n        ],\n    ).map_err(|e| {\n \"Failed to store canary\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": id,\n        \"status\": \"created\"\n    })))\n}\n\n/// Router for canary endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    CanaryState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/canaries\", post(create_canary::<S>))\n        .route(\"/canaries\", get(get_canary_status::<S>))\n        .route(\"/canaries/:id\", get(get_canary::<S>))\n        .route(\"/canaries/:id\",
